quiz.question:
Black is pushing their a-pawn. 

White has two connected passers but the bishop is currently holding them off. 

What should the result of this game be?

answers.text: White is winning because Black's king is too far to defend against the passed pawns.

feedback.text:
Correct! 

Black's king is too far and their a-pawn isn't doing anything. If it ever goes to a2 it will be taken and White can win with the two connected passers.
With the king this far, are you sure this should be a draw?

distractors.text: Black is winning because of the a-pawn.

feedback.text: A win is impossible. If ...a2 is ever played, Bxa2 will be the response and at most Black has a draw.
